hero.headline: One object, many surfaces, one operational identity.
hero.body: Intelligence Workspace keeps selection, compare, and geometry truth aligned across flat map, EO overlay, site 3D, theater 3D, and ops wall views.

proof.headline: Built for continuity instead of disconnected screens.

cards.label: Screen Families
cards.body: Flat map, EO overlay, site 3D, theater 3D, and ops wall all stay inside one workspace model.

cards.label: Selection Spine
cards.body: Selection, compare, and extent state remain centralized instead of being re-invented per surface.

cards.label: Geometry Truth
cards.body: Bounding boxes, projection helpers, and sampled range rings still drive the spatial framing logic.

cards.label: Build Status
cards.body: The current repository ships as a runnable Vite and React prototype instead of a dead handoff page.

feature_section.headline: Each surface answers a different operational question without breaking identity.

items.title: Flat Map
items.body: Truth surface for point, line, and polygon context with bbox-based framing.

items.title: EO Overlay
items.body: Exploitation view for markup, aimpoints, directional cues, and measured context.

items.title: Site 3D
items.body: Local oblique surface for selected-site framing and task-asset context.

items.title: Theater 3D
items.body: Mission geometry surface for rings, route vectors, and tasking relationships.

items.title: Ops Wall
items.body: Command-monitoring surface for clocks, queue panels, and central mission awareness.

workflow_section.headline: The prototype is strongest where interaction stays coherent.
steps: Select an object once and keep it visible across list, surface, compare, and dossier panes.
Switch viewing modes without losing the selected object or the active compare queue.
Change extent mode to reframe by operational, selection, or compare logic using shared bbox math.

demo_section.headline: Explore the current prototype in the same page.
demo_section.body: The interactive workspace below is the actual runnable prototype, not a static mockup.

final_cta.headline: Use the current prototype as the base for the next implementation cycle.
final_cta.body: Review the PRD, inspect the source, or launch directly into the live workspace to continue the repair-first roadmap.
